squad_alert_fingerprint: George Georgiou:Ekitiké (LIV) [INJURED] [bench] — Achilles injury - Expected back 01 Jan
Ed Pragnell:Mitoma (BHA) [DOUBTFUL] [bench] — Hamstring injury - 25% chance of playing

articles.headline: Title Race Settled, Chaos Unfolds for Top Three
articles.body:
Well, let's get straight to the point—Charles has absolutely nailed the title this season, and honestly, it was never in doubt. But the real drama is in the battle for the top three payout places! We’ve got Alexander sitting pretty in second but only 27 points clear of Sam in third, who’s just hanging in there with a decent 54 points this game week. Then there’s David, only 13 points behind Sam and trying to pounce like a cat on a laser dot. It’s genuinely tense stuff, and I can’t wait to see who will come out swinging in this knife-edge tussle!

Now, as for the relegation zone—it’s already a disaster waiting to happen for Joe and Aron, who are miles away from safety. I mean, Joe is 62 points behind, while Aron is 132! It’s like watching a snail race, but the snails have just remembered they’re actually turtles. And don’t forget, with FPL managers sweating over injuries and suspensions, this final week is bound to leave some folks pulling their hair out. It’s unpredictable mayhem—just the way I like it!

articles.headline: Joe and Aron: A Season of Utter Failure
articles.body:
Joe and Aron are going down, no question about it. 62 points and 132 points from safety? Embarrassing. I mean, what have these two been doing all season? It's like they’re taking part in a competition for who can produce the least effort. No excuses for that kind of ineptitude, especially when there’s no chance of catching Ed. Time to pack your bags, lads.

And let’s be honest, the cup final ended up being more exciting than their entire seasons. Dominic bagged a trophy; meanwhile, Joe and Aron are left to reflect on a pitiful finish. I can't believe those two are even professionals. It's painful to watch. You’ve got to ask, is anyone in that relegation scrap even trying?

articles.headline: Dominic's HC XI II Clinches Cup Final Thriller
articles.body:
What a cup final, eh? Dominic's HC XI II edged out Jason's Inevitable by just four points in a tense showdown. I mean, it was like watching a Champions League final unfold, and both managers had their big guns out — Taty with the captain's armband came through, while Jason's Wood just couldn't find the net. It's a massive moment for Dominic, who must be over the moon after that nerve-wracking finish. 

Now, let's talk about the league — with Charles already crowned champion, the real interest lies in the payout race. Alexander, Sam, and David are all within striking distance of securing those sweet, sweet second and third-place prize payouts. It’s tighter than ever, with just 27 points separating Alexander from Sam and David lurking just 13 points behind. And at the other end, poor Joe and Aron are going down like a lead balloon — relegation is a bitter pill to swallow, and they'll be hoping for a miracle next season!

articles.headline: Charles Dominates While Drama Unfolds Below Him
articles.body:
Wow, Charles has made it look easy this season, absolutely unbelievable! But now, the real drama is happening just behind him with Alexander and Sam going at it neck and neck for those precious payout spots. It’s knife-edge stuff! David is still in the mix but only 13 points separates him from third, so he’ll need to pull something spectacular out of the bag. You’ve got to give them some credit, though; the pressure is high and, with only one match left, every point is vital!

Now, looking at the bottom, it’s a sad state for Joe and Aron, really! Joe is 62 points adrift and Aron’s way off the pace, 132 points behind safety. It’s like watching a car crash in slow motion! Meanwhile, Ed's sitting pretty just above the drop zone, and with Mitoma doubtful, he’s got to hope that the rest of his squad can drag him over the line. It's crazy how injuries can flip the script at this stage! What a season we’ve had!
